---
title: "Some Spatial Basics"
author: "..."
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
options(tidyverse.quiet = TRUE)
```



## For your final project, we'll include some cartographic visualization.

Background, sf objects ...

> Simple Features (officially Simple Feature Access) is a set of standards that specify a common storage and access model of geographic features made of mostly two-dimensional geometries (point, line, polygon, multi-point, multi-line, etc.) used by geographic databases and geographic information systems. It is formalized by both the Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC) and the International Organization for Standardization (ISO). - Wikipedia en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simple_Features



![sf visual explanation](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/520851/50280460-e35c1880-044c-11e9-9ed7-cc46754e49db.jpg)



### Part 1. Some basics

https://r-spatial.org/r/2018/10/25/ggplot2-sf.html

```{r}
options(scipen = 10)

library(tidyverse)

library("rnaturalearth")
library("rnaturalearthdata")

world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

world |> select(1:5, geometry)


## classic usage..
world |> 
  ggplot() +
  aes(geometry = geometry) +
  geom_sf()
```

# Does ggplot2 stuff work? Change the labs to longitud and latitude

```{r}
last_plot() + 
  labs(x = "Hello!",
       y = "Yes!",
       title = "I am told there are people who don't care for maps \n but I find that hard to believe",
       subtitle = "Robert Louis Stevenson")
```

# Does *set* fill color work?  Change the color that's provided to one of your choosing

```{r}
last_plot() + 
  aes(fill = I("green"))
```


# Does mapping fill color work? 

```{r}
last_plot() +
  aes(fill = pop_est)
```


# Do scale adjustments work?  Change the scale to another one that you prefer, (cividis, inferno, magma, plasma)

```{r}
last_plot() + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis")


```


# Here we might also want to adjust color taking the log...


```{r}
last_plot() + 
  aes(fill = pop_est |> log10())
```


# Here is another approach

```{r}
last_plot() + 
  aes(fill = pop_est) + # back to 'straight' mapping
  scale_fill_viridis_c(transform = scales::log10_trans()) 
```


## Part II. get your WDI data... map it?



### Step 1 A. get data w/ WDI package

```{r, cache=TRUE}
library(WDI)
wdi_binding <-  WDI(country= "all", 
                 indicator= c("TM.TAX.TCOM.BC.ZS"),
                 start=1996, end=1996, # choose a single year
                 extra = T)

head(wdi_binding)  
```

### B. Step 1 B. bit of cleaning

```{r}
wdi_binding_clean <- wdi_binding |> 
  filter_out(region == "Aggregates") |>
  rename(binding = TM.TAX.TCOM.BC.ZS)

head(wdi_binding_clean)
```


### Step 2. A. declare reference data

```{r}
#######
## in console:  remotes::install_github(EvaMaeRey/ggregions)
######

library(ggregions)

# set up - declare your regions of interest with unique identifiers
world_ref_data <- world |> select(sovereignt, iso_a3, iso_a2, geometry)

world_ref_data

options(ggregions.ref.regions = world_ref_data)
```


### Step 3: Map World bank indicators data...

```{r}
wdi_binding_clean |>
  ggplot() + 
  aes(region = country) + 
  geom_region()

last_plot() + 
  aes(fill = binding)
```

# highlighting a particular country...

```{r}
last_plot() + 
  stamp_region_border(keep = "Australia", color = "yellow")
```





# Part III. One more 'homemade dataset' exercise.  

Change the collection of countries to illuminate a different question (here a map that supports security council expansion ideas.)

How do the two maps differ? Which bit of syntax is responsible?

>

```{r, warning=F, fig.show='hold', out.width="49%" }

# plot1
tribble(~country, 
        "Germany",
        "India",
        "Japan",
        "Brazil") |> 
  ggplot() + 
  aes(region = country) + 
  geom_region(fill = "darkblue")

# plot2
tribble(~country, 
        "Germany",
        "India",
        "Japan",
        "Brazil") |> 
  ggplot() + 
  aes(region = country) + 
  stamp_region() + 
  geom_region(fill = "darkblue") + 
  labs(title = "Security Council Expansion?")
```


# Part IV.  Howe is the syntax different when we *don't* use ggregions? 

> A couple of observations...

```{r}
wdi_binding_clean |> 
  full_join(world, by = join_by(iso3c == iso_a3)) |> 
  ggplot() + 
  aes(geometry = geometry, 
      fill = binding) +
  geom_sf() + 
  geom_sf(data = . %>% filter(country == "Australia"),
          color = "goldenrod")


# aes(filter = country == "Australia") - coming soon to ggplot2 
```




## Closing remarks, Other Relevant Work, Caveats
